stimulus: The 2019 Nobel Prize in Physics was shared by three contributors to modern cosmology: theoretical pioneer James Peebles, who developed foundational models of large-scale structure in the 1960s; Didier Queloz, codiscoverer of the first exoplanet orbiting a sunlike star at the Observatory of ______blank and Michel Mayor, an emeritus professor at the same observatory.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. This is a complex list whose items already contain internal commas ("who developed...1960s"; "codiscoverer...sunlike star..."; "an emeritus professor...observatory"). Semicolons must therefore separate the items.

Choice A is incorrect because a comma cannot separate items in a list whose items already contain internal commas. Choice B is incorrect because some punctuation is needed to mark the boundary between the second and third list items. Choice D is incorrect because colons are not used to separate items in a list.
